message:
refactor(ft-autograd): improve formatting and simplify test mutation patterns

Two changes:

1. Formatting: the destructured tuple from index_select's metadata
   extraction was squeezed onto a single line, making it hard to read
   when scanning diffs. Break it across multiple lines to align with
   the project style for multi-field destructuring.

2. Test pattern: the sort and topk backward-rejection tests used a
   `match ... { variant => mutate, other => panic!(...) }` pattern
   to corrupt internal indices before asserting that backward rejects
   them. Replace with `assert!(matches!(...))` followed by an
   `if let` mutation block. This is clearer about intent: the assert
   proves the node is the right variant, and the if-let performs the
   surgical mutation. It also avoids the catch-all panic arm, which
   could silently mask future enum variant additions.

No behavioral changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
